message:
fix(ffs-alloc): detect double-free of inodes in bitmap allocator

Previously, calling free_inode on an inode number that was already
marked as free in the group descriptor bitmap would silently succeed,
clearing a bit that was already zero. This could mask serious
filesystem corruption bugs where the same inode is freed twice --
for example, during error recovery paths or rename-over-existing
operations that interact with unlink.

Add an explicit check before bitmap_clear: if the bit for the
target inode is already zero, return an FfsError::Corruption with
a descriptive "double-free" message that includes the inode number.
This turns a silent data-corruption scenario into an immediate,
diagnosable error, making it much easier to catch and fix the root
cause in higher-level code paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
